title: event — authorization policies
description: Two policies. EventPolicy narrows by scope suffix + visibility (a caller can only see events at or below their role's visibility ceiling — guardians see participants_only + public; members see tenant_only + up; admins see everything). EventFacilityPolicy piggybacks on the parent event's policy (can-manage-event ⇒ can-manage-facilities).

abilities.scope: Row-level visibility filter: guardians+athletes see visibility IN (public, participants_only [when they're registered]); members+coach+reception see visibility IN (public, tenant_only, participants_only [when registered]); admin+owner see everything. Base permission narrowed via ScopeSuffixResolver.
Admin + owner only. Required to see visibility='private' events.
Admin or owner. Also gated by sports_events entitlement + event_slot per-year cap.
Admin or owner. Mutable fields depend on status — see routes.json PATCH endpoint. Post-registration_open changes to dates/venue/pricing/eligibility require the specific sub-permissions.
Owner only. Post-registration_open price changes. Audit-critical.
Owner only. Post-registration_open min_age/max_age/age_group_ids narrowing that would exclude existing registrants.
Owner only. Soft-delete (archive). Refused when active registrations reference.
Admin or owner. Fires cannot-opt-out cancellation broadcast.
Admin or owner. Gated by event_public_visibility entitlement.
Admin or owner. Gated by event_livestream entitlement.
platform_admin only + super_admin approval required per action-level middleware.
Piggybacks on the parent event's visibility. If the caller can view the event, they can list its facilities.
Admin or owner. Multi-facility gated by event_multi_facility entitlement.
